calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Paga Settlement And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. KYLE R. NORDREHAUG, Esq. of Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP (Tel: 858-551-1223) for Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DION MARTIN's Motion To Approve PAGA Settlement.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to approve PAGA settlement is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code 2699(l)(2); see Haralson v. U.S. Aviation Services Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 383 F.Supp.3d 959, 972; Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.) The Court also finds that the proposed award of $366,666 in attorneys' fees, which represents 33% of the gross settlement amount of $1.1 million, and $53,612.33 in costs, is a reasonable amount in the circumstances of the case, including the result achieved, contingent risk, and counsel's lodestar. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half Int'l Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed For Defendants Failure To Comply With The March 7, 2019 Court Order; Memorandum In Support
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ruchira P. Mukherjee, Esq. of BLUMENTHAL, NORDREHAUG & BHOWMIK, T:858.551.1223, ext.7, for plaintiff and moving party, on Court Call;
Archana Manwani, Esq., of FOLEY & LARDNER LLP, T:213.972.4500, for defendant and opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: Parties were required to appear regarding status of discovery. Hearing was held. After hearing, the Court ruled on PLAINTIFF DION MARTIN's Motion For An Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed For Defendants Failure To Comply With The March 7, 2019 Court Order, as follows:

Defendant shall produce all remaining outstanding records, electronic and otherwise, as reflected in the March 7, 2019 court order, no later than September 13, 2019. Further, plaintiff is awarded monetary sanctions for bringing the instant motion, the amount to be determined upon the filing of a declaration by plaintiff's counsel. Defendant is admonished that should it fail to comply, the Court can issue evidentiary or terminating sanctions against the defendant.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order reflecting the Court's ruling, as fully reported in the court's official transcript.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
